question: With the FULL clonal mutation repertoire (not just shared hotspots), what fraction of patients have >=1 clean tumour-exclusive neoantigen handle to SEED the death wave?

exome_model.tmb_note: median mut/Mb (TCGA/PCAWG order-of-magnitude); high-TMB driven by UV(melanoma)/smoking(NSCLC)/MMR-d(MSI).

INTERPRETATION_MAP.shared_vs_personalised: hotspot coverage (RUNG-11) = SHARED off-the-shelf targets (bounded). P_ge1 here = PER-PATIENT addressability with PERSONALISED neoantigen ID + a per-patient effector. The gap is the shared-vs-personalised cost.
INTERPRETATION_MAP.seed_sufficiency: >=1 CLONAL clean handle is enough to SEED the RUNG-13 wave (efficacy decoupled from per-cell recognition); high-TMB cancers -> nearly every patient seedable.
INTERPRETATION_MAP.tcr_residual: presentation != immunogenicity; per-handle TCR existence is the wet-lab residual (RUNG-12), un-pre-validatable for private mutations.

DECISIVE: Deployable clean clonal neoantigens per mutation: literature band [0.01,0.05], MHCflurry-calibrated 0.117 (strong-binder, per-register WT-clean test, 6-allele patient). PER-PATIENT P(>=1 clean clonal neoantigen to SEED the wave), shown as range [lit-low .. lit-high]: CRC_MSI 99%-100%, MELANOMA 81%-100%, NSCLC 64%-99%, BLADDER 64%-99%, GASTRIC 53%-98%, HNSC 47%-96%, HCC 40%-92%, CRC_MSS 36%-89%, GLIOMA 27%-79%, OV 27%-79%, PDAC 20%-68%, BRCA 18%-64%. Robustly SOLVED per-patient (>=90% even at the CONSERVATIVE 1% yield): ['CRC_MSI']; hard (<50% even at 5% yield): none. This is PERSONALISED addressability (the patient has a clean clonal handle to SEED the RUNG-13 wave, which then spreads) -- NOT an off-the-shelf drug, which stays bounded to the shared HOTSPOT ceiling (RUNG-11: melanoma 20%, PDAC 26%). The shared-vs-personalised gap is the cost. TCR/immunogenicity per handle = the wet-lab residual (RUNG-12); presentation != killing.
calibration_note: MHCflurry-calibrated deployable rate (0.117) sits ABOVE the literature band [0.01,0.05] because the simulation scores raw MHC binding only -- it omits the proteasomal-cleavage + TAP-transport + processing filters that real immunopeptidomics applies (which remove most predicted binders). So the HEADLINE uses the literature band; MHCflurry is the optimistic upper bound.
CEILING: PREDICTED presentation (MHCflurry), not immunopeptidomics/immunogenicity -> calibration is an UPPER bound (no processing filter); headline uses the conservative literature yield band. TMB + clonal_frac are population estimates (literature ranges). Simulated missense spectrum approximates real. P_ge1 = per-patient addressability requiring PERSONALISED neoantigen ID + effector, NOT an off-the-shelf drug. Bounds addressability, not efficacy; coupling/TCR = wet-lab residual.
